regime_justification: USDJPY fell sharply 2.38% to 156.57 after BOJ April meeting, three dissenters voted for hike, oil at $105+, yen breaking 160 triggered near-term unwind dynamics

domino_stage.label: Shock / Trigger
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY broke above 160 on Apr-29, triggering sharp JPY rally to 156.57 today; vol spike, three BOJ dissenters signaled hawkish shift

1w.label: Likely
1w.driver: USDJPY unwound 2.4% in 24h, 160 breach triggered reversal, MOF intervention risk elevated, oil $105+ sustains pressure

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Unwind momentum may pause; BOJ next meeting June, spread still 187bps wide; carry-to-vol collapsed but not disorderly yet

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Iran ceasefire talks ongoing; if oil retreats below $95, yen pressure eases; otherwise fragile setup persists

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Time for policy stabilization; unless sustained oil >$110 or second BOJ hike, acute unwind risk fades by late May

bottom_line: USDJPY crashed 2.4% to 156.57 after briefly piercing 160 yesterday, triggering Stage-3 shock dynamics. Three BOJ dissenters voted for immediate hike, WTI at $105, and JGB 10Y spiked to 2.53%. Carry unwind is actively in progress, but not yet capitulation. Next 5 days critical: watch for MOF rate-checks, further yen strength toward 150, and HY credit widening.

narrative.p1: Overnight the carry trade thesis moved from Stage 1 (Pressure Building) to Stage 3 (Shock/Trigger) as USDJPY briefly pierced 160.37 before collapsing 2.38% to 156.57 by NY morning. The catalyst: BOJ's April-28 hold at 0.75% was accompanied by three of nine board members voting for an immediate hike—the highest dissent since 1992—and a risk scenario forecasting core inflation at 3.1% in FY2026 if oil stays above $105. WTI crude spiked to $111 intraday Wednesday before settling at $107, then pulled back to $105 today as Trump rejected Iran's latest Hormuz proposal. JGB 10Y yield surged 6.5 bps to 2.53%, a 25-year high. Fed held rates as expected but also saw three dissenters opposing the easing bias, pushing US 10Y to 4.42%. The combo—BOJ hawkish pivot + sustained energy shock + 160 breach—triggered acute unwind. Regime upgraded from yellow to red. Stage moved from 1 to 3.
narrative.p2: The quant anchors confirm the shift. US-JP spread compressed from ~200 bps Tuesday to 189 bps today (US 10Y 4.42% minus JGB 2.53%). USDJPY 1M implied vol spiked from ~7% to ~8.5%, collapsing the carry-to-vol ratio from 27 to 22—still positive but eroding fast. USDJPY's 5-day move is -2.4%, well above MOF's historical pace threshold for verbal intervention. Distance from 60d mean: USDJPY was 159.82 Monday, now 156.57—likely stretched to the downside short-term, but the 60d mean itself has been drifting higher (~155), so we're near fair value post-unwind. The key: pace and volatility, not absolute level. 1-week grade B (Likely) reflects active unwind signatures—sharp FX move, vol spike, three BOJ dissenters, oil $105+. 2-week grade C (Watchful) because the unwind may pause: next BOJ meeting is June, spread is still wide, and MOVE/VIX are not signaling systemic cascade yet. Applied the unwind prior as a tiebreaker for 2w—data is mixed (unwind in FX, but equities/credit still orderly), so lean one notch more alert: would otherwise grade D, upgraded to C. 3-week and 4-week grades C and D respectively, weighted by scenario: if oil retreats and Iran deal progresses, acute risk fades; if oil sustains >$110, we're in Stage 4 forced liquidation.
narrative.p3: BTC at $76,095 is down only 0.2% despite the FX vol spike and equity selloff in Japan. XRP at ~$1.37-$1.39 range, flat to slightly down. This decoupling is notable: crypto should drain in a classic risk-off unwind, yet BTC has held $75k-$77k for weeks. Two reads: (1) Crypto liquidity is structurally independent of yen carry now—different marginal buyer (US institutions, ETFs). (2) Lag effect—if HY credit widens or equities crack further, BTC follows with a 1-3 day delay. For positioning: treat BTC stability as a yellow flag, not green. If Nikkei breaks below 58k or HY spreads widen >50bps in a week, expect crypto to give back 5-10%. If BTC breaks below $72k, that's confirmation the unwind is broadening beyond FX into levered risk assets. For now, crypto is a bystander, not a participant—but that can flip fast.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 grade B reflects the active shock: USDJPY unwound 2.4% in 24h, three BOJ dissenters, oil $105+, JGB at 2.53%. Unwind is in progress. Week 2 grade C acknowledges that the initial shock may stabilize—no BOJ meeting until June, MOF may verbally intervene to cap yen strength, and oil could ease if Iran deal progresses. The unwind prior was applied here: data is genuinely ambiguous (FX unwinding, but credit/equities not cascading), so we lean watchful rather than unlikely.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 to 3: if the unwind stabilizes (USDJPY 150-155 range, oil retreats to $95-$100), we remain at C (Watchful). If oil stays >$105 and yen strengthens further toward 150, we escalate to B (Likely) as Stage 4 forced liquidation begins. If Iran ceasefire resolves and oil drops below $90, we de-escalate to D (Unlikely) as the acute trigger dissipates.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 to 4: scenario-weighted. By late May, either (a) policy stabilization occurs—BOJ rhetoric calms markets, MOF intervenes, oil normalizes—and acute unwind risk fades (grade D), or (b) a second shock (BOJ surprise hike, sustained oil >$110, HY credit widening) pushes us into Stage 4-5 (grades B-A). The base case is stabilization, hence D for week 4, but the tail risk is non-trivial.

watch_list: MOF verbal intervention or rate-checks in next 48h (watch for yen to pause rally at 155 or reverse toward 158)
BOJ June meeting pricing: if market prices >50% hike probability, yen strengthens further and carry unwind accelerates
Oil: if WTI breaks below $95, acute imported-inflation pressure eases; if sustains >$110, Stage 4 forced liquidation likely
HY credit spreads: any widening >30bps in a week signals contagion from FX to credit; watch HYG/JNK or CDX HY
BTC below $72k would confirm unwind broadening to levered crypto; Nikkei below 58k signals equity stress amplifying
